name: Aniline
plain_summary: Amino-benzene; building block for dyes. UV bands red-shift vs benzene.

spectra.plain_caption: UV absorption; pure aniline is pale — dyes made from it are colorful.

source.citation: UV absorption; pure aniline is pale — dyes made from it are colorful.
source.license: CC-BY-4.0 packaging; verify experimental values against primary literature for research use.

spectra.plain_caption: IR fingerprint: N–H ~3380; N–H bend ~1620. Peaks mark how bonds stretch and bend.

source.citation: Educational multi-Gaussian IR envelope from typical functional-group wavenumbers (not a raw FTIR file).

spectra.plain_caption: Raman shifts: ring ~1600; ring breathing ~1000. Raman highlights polarizable bonds (teaching envelope).

source.citation: Educational multi-Gaussian Raman envelope from typical shifts (not a raw spectrometer file).
Teaching ¹H/¹³C chemical shifts after standard organic spectroscopy tables (Pretsch / Pavia-style compilations). Multiplet envelopes are simulated from δ and J — not raw FIDs or instrument SI.
